title: AgentID + APS Interop Test Vector
description: Schema for interop test vectors in fixtures/{agentid,aps,agentgraph,composed}/v1/ and forward. Supports both structural-shape (v1) and cryptographically-verifiable (v2) fixture forms. Three input signals are now recognized: agentid_attestation (identity), aps_delegation (authorization), agentgraph_scan (security posture). Composed envelopes carry any two or all three.

scenario.description: Kebab-case identifier for the test vector. Must be unique within its directory.

description.description: Single-sentence human-readable description of what the vector tests.

subject.description: The agent the vector is about.

did.description: Full W3C DID for the subject.

bound_addresses.description: Wallet addresses bound to this agent (when wallet binding is in scope).

inputs.description: System-specific input envelopes. At least one of agentid_attestation or aps_delegation must be present.

agentid_attestation.description: AgentID attestation envelope (verify response, trust-header payload, or attestation envelope).

aps_delegation.description: APS delegation envelope. Two shapes are tolerated. Legacy (pre-v1-structural): flat delegator/delegate/scope/spend_limit/wallet_ref/not_before/not_after/nonce, used by the earliest APS interop fixtures. v1-structural (published shape: aps-v2-structural — the version string names the APS crate version, not the envelope schema generation): delegation_chain array with per-hop delegator_did/delegate_did/scope/not_before/not_after, a delegation_chain_root sha256 hash over the JCS-canonicalized chain, issuer_did pinned to did:web:gateway.aeoess.com, and canonicalization_spec. v2 will add signed_form alongside AgentID and AgentGraph in lockstep.

version.description: Legacy: semver (e.g. '1.40.0'). v1-structural: 'aps-v2-structural' (the version string tracks the APS crate, not the envelope generation).

subject_did.description: DID of the subject the delegation is about — typically shared with the AgentID and AgentGraph slots in a composed envelope.

issuer_did.description: APS issuer DID. Stable — external verifiers pin to this value.

delegation_chain.description: Ordered list of delegation hops, index 0 being the principal grant and each subsequent hop sub-delegating a scope that MUST be a subset of its parent (monotonic narrowing). A revocation entry on any ancestor hop invalidates the whole chain (cascade revocation).

delegation_chain_root.description: SHA-256 over the JCS-canonicalized delegation_chain array. Format: 'sha256:<64 hex chars>'. Consumers MUST independently recompute and verify before trusting chain contents.

agentgraph_scan.description: AgentGraph security-scan slot (published shape: agentgraph-scan-v1-structural). Third signal in the composed-v1 envelope — attests to code-level security posture of the subject's linked artifact (repo, package, MCP server, or API endpoint). Structural-shape in v1; v2 will add signed_form alongside AgentID and APS in lockstep.

version.description: Slot schema version. v1-structural for shape-only vectors, v1-signed when signed_form is present.

subject_did.description: DID of the subject the scan is about — typically shared with the AgentID slot so all three slots in a composed envelope attest about the same agent.

issuer_did.description: AgentGraph issuer DID. Stable — external verifiers pin to this value.

artifact_ref.description: Content-addressed reference (e.g. 'git:sha256:<commit>', 'pkg:npm@1.2.3'). Empty string allowed when scanner did not record a digest.

gates.description: Three published security gates for v1. v2 will add identity_anchor, capability_manifest, runtime_attestation.

overall_grade.description: Proprietary composite — consumers MAY treat as opaque. The gate-level grades/scores are the interop surface.

evidence_hash.description: SHA-256 over the JCS-canonicalized evidence payload. Format: 'sha256:<64 hex chars>'.

signed_form.description: Cryptographic verification material. Optional in v1 (structural-only). REQUIRED in v2.

jws.description: Compact JWS representation of the signed envelope.

payload_canonical.description: Hex or base64url representation of the JCS-canonicalized payload bytes that were signed.

signature.description: Hex or base64url representation of the raw signature bytes.

public_key.description: Public key for offline signature verification.

jwks_url.description: Public JWKS URL where the signing key can be resolved at verification time.

kid.description: Key identifier matching the entry in jwks_url.

expected_result.description: Expected gate states, failing gates, and composite decision a conformant verifier should produce. Five-gate model per the converged design (A2A #1672): per-gate failure reporting prevents collapse into a single confidence score, and failing_gates names exactly which gate(s) tipped the verdict.

identity_gate.description: AgentID identity gate state. 'passed' means DID resolves and identity is current; 'failed' means unresolvable or invalid; 'stale' means past freshness window but DID still resolves; 'not_applicable' means this vector intentionally does not test identity (must be explicit, not omitted).

delegation_gate.description: APS delegation gate state. 'passed' means delegation in window with valid scope; 'failed' means revoked, expired, or scope-mismatched; 'not_applicable' means vector does not test delegation.

wallet_state_gate.description: Wallet state gate. 'passed' means the bound wallet matches the signed payload claim; 'failed' means divergence (e.g., AgentID solana_address != APS wallet_ref); 'not_applicable' means vector does not test wallet binding.

revocation_gate.description: Revocation gate. 'passed' means no revocation present at evaluation time; 'failed' means a revocation entry preceded the request (CRED_REVOKED_AT_PROCESS or equivalent); 'not_applicable' means vector does not test revocation.

policy_gate.description: Policy gate. 'passed' means action satisfies the active constraint set; 'failed' means action violates a scope, spend, or other policy constraint; 'not_applicable' means vector does not test policy.

key_lifecycle_gate.description: AgentID key lifecycle gate state. Richer enum than the strict pass/fail gates because the diagnostic distinction between 'rotated', 'revoked', and 'compromised' carries different consumer responses (e.g., 'compromised' invalidates ALL prior receipts, 'rotated' does not).

security_gate.description: AgentGraph security gate state — the AND-composition of the three published AgentGraph gates (static_analysis, secret_scan, dependency_audit). 'passed' means all three sub-gates clean; 'failed' means at least one sub-gate failed; 'not_applicable' means vector does not test the security dimension. Sub-gate diagnosis lives inside inputs.agentgraph_scan.gates so consumers know exactly which dimension failed.

composite_decision.description: The final decision a verifier should produce given the AND-composition of all gates that were evaluated. AND-composition means a single failed gate produces deny regardless of how many other gates passed.

failing_gates.description: Array of gate names that failed (or returned a non-passing state). Empty array when composite_decision is 'permit'. Non-empty when composite_decision is 'deny' / 'conditional_allow' / 'provisional_allow' / 'refer'. This is the structural encoding of per-gate failure reporting — a verifier that produces the right composite for the wrong reason fails the vector.

reasoning.description: Human-readable explanation of why the composite_decision was reached.

decisive_gate.description: Which gate was the deciding factor in the composite decision. Convenience field — derivable from failing_gates but useful for human readers.

metadata.description: Vector metadata for catalog and cross-validation.

schema_version.description: Version of vector.schema.json this fixture conforms to.

fixture_form.description: Whether this fixture is structural-shape only (v1) or includes cryptographically verifiable bytes (v2).

contributors.description: GitHub handles of contributors to this fixture.
